data.command: FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""); CONTENT=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$ ]] && echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q 'console\.log'; then echo 'Warning: console.log statements should be removed before committing' >&2; exit 2; fi
FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" == "package.json" ]]; then echo 'Checking for vulnerable dependencies...'; npm audit --audit-level=moderate; fi
FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$ ]]; then npx prettier --write "$FILE"; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(ts|tsx)$ ]]; then RESULT=$(npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1); if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then echo "TypeScript errors found: $RESULT" >&2; exit 2; fi; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$ ]] && grep -q 'import \* from' "$FILE"; then echo 'Warning: Avoid wildcard imports for better tree-shaking' >&2; exit 2; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$ && "$FILE" != *".test."* && "$FILE" != *".spec."* ]]; then DIR=$(dirname "$FILE"); BASENAME=$(basename "$FILE" | sed -E 's/\.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$//'); for TEST_FILE in "$DIR/$BASENAME.test."* "$DIR/$BASENAME.spec."*; do if [ -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then echo "Running tests for $TEST_FILE..."; if command -v jest >/dev/null 2>&1; then npx jest "$TEST_FILE" --passWithNoTests; elif command -v vitest >/dev/null 2>&1; then npx vitest run "$TEST_FILE"; fi; break; fi; done; fi

input.content:
-- ============================================================
-- AstroLab â€” 008_rls_hardening.sql
-- RLS hardening based on security audit
-- ============================================================

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- FIX 1 (CRITICAL): Remove wide-open qr_codes SELECT policy
-- Replace with secure RPC function for token lookup
-- -------------------------------------------------------
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS qr_codes_select_by_token ON qr_codes;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.lookup_qr_by_token(qr_token TEXT)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id UUID,
  merchant_id UUID,
  qr_type TEXT,
  campaign_id UUID,
  is_active BOOLEAN,
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  max_uses INTEGER,
  uses_count INTEGER
) AS $$
  SELECT q.id, q.merchant_id, q.qr_type::TEXT, q.campaign_id, q.is_active, q.expires_at, q.max_uses, q.uses_count
  FROM qr_codes q
  WHERE q.token = qr_token
    AND q.is_active = true
    AND (q.expires_at IS NULL OR q.expires_at > now())
    AND (q.max_uses IS NULL OR q.uses_count < q.max_uses)
$$ LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- FIX 2 (MEDIUM): Restrict rewards visibility to linked customers
-- Merchants still see all their own rewards via rewards_select_merchant_all
-- -------------------------------------------------------
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS rewards_select_active ON rewards;

CREATE POLICY rewards_select_customer_linked ON rewards
  FOR SELECT USING (
    is_active = true
    AND (
      -- Merchant sees all their own rewards
      merchant_id = auth.merchant_id()
      OR
      -- Customer sees rewards only from merchants they have a card with
      merchant_id IN (
        SELECT lc.merchant_id FROM loyalty_cards lc
        WHERE lc.customer_id = auth.customer_id()
      )
    )
  );

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- FIX 3 (MINOR): Add WITH CHECK to ai_campaign_suggestions UPDATE
-- -------------------------------------------------------
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Merchants can update their suggestions" ON ai_campaign_suggestions;

CREATE POLICY "Merchants can update their suggestions"
  ON ai_campaign_suggestions FOR UPDATE
  USING (merchant_id = auth.merchant_id())
  WITH CHECK (merchant_id = auth.merchant_id());

input.content:
-- ============================================================
-- AstroLab â€” RLS Audit Script
-- Run: psql $DATABASE_URL -f scripts/audit-rls.sql
-- ============================================================

-- 1. List ALL tables and their RLS status
SELECT
  schemaname, tablename,
  CASE WHEN rowsecurity THEN 'âś… RLS ON' ELSE 'âťŚ RLS OFF' END as rls_status
FROM pg_tables
WHERE schemaname = 'public'
ORDER BY tablename;

-- 2. List all policies by table
SELECT
  tablename, policyname, permissive, roles, cmd, qual, with_check
FROM pg_policies
WHERE schemaname = 'public'
ORDER BY tablename, policyname;

-- 3. ALERT: Tables with RLS enabled but NO policies
SELECT t.tablename AS "âš ď¸Ź Tables with RLS but NO policies"
FROM pg_tables t
LEFT JOIN pg_policies p ON t.tablename = p.tablename AND p.schemaname = 'public'
WHERE t.schemaname = 'public' AND t.rowsecurity = true
GROUP BY t.tablename
HAVING COUNT(p.policyname) = 0;

-- 4. ALERT: Tables with overly permissive policies (USING true)
SELECT tablename, policyname, cmd AS "âš ď¸Ź Overly permissive policies (USING true)"
FROM pg_policies
WHERE schemaname = 'public' AND qual = 'true'
ORDER BY tablename;

-- 5. Tables WITHOUT RLS (should be empty)
SELECT tablename AS "âťŚ Tables WITHOUT RLS"
FROM pg_tables
WHERE schemaname = 'public' AND NOT rowsecurity
ORDER BY tablename;
